title: Brightness and Contrast Jitter
concept: Brightness and contrast jitter randomly shift how light and how punchy a training image looks, simulating different times of day and exposure settings so a CNN doesn't overfit to one lighting condition.

exercises.prompt: Recap: Photometric augmentation changes an image's pixel values instead of its pixel positions. Which pair below is a photometric change?

options.text: Making an image darker and washing out its contrast
Rotating an image 90 degrees and flipping it
Cropping an image and resizing it

exercises.explain: Right — darkness and contrast are value changes. Now let's zoom into exactly how brightness and contrast jitter work.

cards.title: Noon sun vs. golden hour
cards.body: Photograph your dog at noon under harsh sunlight, then again at golden hour with soft, dim light. Same dog, same pose — but one photo is bright and washed out, the other is warm and dim. A model trained only on noon photos might struggle at dusk.

cards.title: Brightness jitter and contrast jitter
cards.body: Brightness jitter randomly shifts how light or dark an entire image looks, adding or subtracting from every pixel's value. Contrast jitter randomly stretches or shrinks the gap between the image's darkest and lightest pixels, making it look punchier or flatter.

cards.title: Turning random knobs on an old TV
cards.body: Imagine an old television with physical brightness and contrast knobs. Brightness jitter is like randomly nudging the brightness knob before each training image; contrast jitter is like randomly nudging the contrast knob. Small, random turns — not extremes.

cards.title: One traffic sign, several exposures
cards.body: A self-driving car dataset takes one photo of a stop sign and creates several training copies: one brightened to simulate midday glare, one darkened to simulate an overcast afternoon, one with boosted contrast to simulate a crisp clear day. The model learns to recognize the sign under all of them.

cards.title: Jitter isn't about faking night from day
cards.body: It's tempting to think brightness jitter could turn a daytime photo into a fake nighttime one. In practice, jitter uses a small, realistic random range — enough to cover normal exposure variation, not enough to invent impossible lighting that never happens in real photos.

cards.title: Brightness jitter vs. contrast jitter
cards.body: Two related but different value shifts:

left.text:
Shifts overall lightness up or down
Simulates dim room vs. bright sun
Contrast spread stays the same

right.text:
Stretches or shrinks light-dark spread
Simulates hazy vs. crisp exposure
Average brightness stays the same

exercises.prompt: What does brightness jitter do to a training image?

options.text: Randomly shifts the overall lightness of the whole image up or down
Randomly rotates the image a few degrees
Randomly crops a corner off the image

exercises.explain: Right — brightness jitter shifts how light or dark the whole image looks.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Contrast jitter changes how bright the image looks on average, not just the spread between its darkest and lightest pixels.
exercises.explain: False. Contrast jitter stretches or shrinks the spread between dark and light pixels; the average brightness stays roughly the same.

exercises.sentence: Brightness and contrast jitter simulate different ___ conditions and exposure settings without moving any content.
exercises.explain: Jitter mimics real-world lighting and exposure variation, like sun vs. shade or a hazy vs. crisp camera.

exercises.prompt: Which of these best describes contrast jitter applied to a photo?

options.text: The dark areas get a little darker and the light areas get a little lighter (or the reverse), same content, same position
The photo gets rotated 20 degrees clockwise

exercises.explain: Correct — that stretching or shrinking of the light-dark spread is exactly what contrast jitter does.

exercises.prompt: A model trained only on well-lit, studio-style product photos performs poorly on customer photos taken in dim rooms or under harsh flash. What's the most direct photometric fix?

options.text: Add brightness and contrast jitter during training so the model sees darker, lighter, flatter, and punchier versions of the same products
Add more geometric augmentation like rotation and cropping, since lighting is a positional problem
Remove all augmentation so the model memorizes the exact studio lighting
Only collect more studio photos taken under the exact same lights

exercises.explain: Exactly! Brightness and contrast jitter directly simulate the exposure variation the model will face in the real world. Next: jittering color and hue, not just light and dark.
